name: Czech A2 Vocabulary
description: Elementary Czech — CEFR A2 level vocabulary

facts.explanation: novinka — news (new information of interest). Also: innovation
pád — fall (move to a lower position). Also: descent (into a lower status or condition), case
papež — pope (head of the Roman Catholic Church).
plán — plan (drawing showing technical details of an artifact). Also: plan (set of intended actions aimed at a goal)
plocha — area (measure of squared distance). Also: desktop (on-screen background)
polovina — half (one of two equal parts into which something may be divided).
ročník — year (a level or grade at school or college).
sníh — snow. Also: whipped egg whites, cocaine
termín — date. Also: deadline, term (word or phrase, especially one from a specialised area of knowledge)
turné — tour (of performances, exhibition games etc).
vlasy — hair (mass of filaments growing from the skin of the head of humans).
volný — free (not imprisoned). Also: free, free of charge (obtainable without payment), free (unconstrained, not bound), loose
vůz — cart. Also: car, automobile, car (railroad car; railway carriage), carriage (on rails)
východ — exit (from a building). Also: east, sunrise
základ — base (something from which other things extend; a foundation). Also: base (number raised to the power of an exponent), basic (a necessary commodity, a staple requirement)
zámek — lock (something used for fastening). Also: château, castle (French-style castle), palace, manor house
zeď — wall (in a building, around a city).
zlato — gold. Also: darling, or, gold
značka — make (brand or kind of a manufactured product). Also: sign (traffic sign), tag (in HTML or XML)
barva — color. Also: paint (liquid), ink (printing), suit (in card games), flush, color, tincture, blood (of hunted animals and hunting dogs)
blok — block. Also: notebook (a book in which notes or memoranda are written)
bod — point. Also: item (of an agenda), point, mark, stab
Čína — China (a region and country in East Asia; capital: Beijing).
čistý — clean. Also: pure (of a branch of science), neat, perfect (describing an interval)

deska — board (relatively long, wide and thin piece of sawn wood or similar material, usually intended for use in construction). Also: plate, disk (flat, round/square piece of some material), record, platter (device for recording sound and playing back on a record player), log, register
fáze — phase (like phases of the moon). Also: phase (phase of matter), phase (initial angle of a sinusoid function at its origin)
frank — franc (any of several units of currency such as Swiss franc). Also: franc (former currency of France and some other countries)
hlas — voice (sound uttered by the mouth). Also: vote (formalized choice on matters of administration or other democratic activities)
hod — throw. Also: feast day, holy day
hodnota — value (that renders something desirable or valuable). Also: value (numerical quantity)
chyba — fault. Also: mistake, error, bug, error
Indie — India (a country in South Asia).
Itálie — Italy (a country in Southern Europe).
kámen — stone (substance, small piece). Also: piece (in board games)
koncept — draft (early version of a written work).
kus — piece (either "part" or as a counter word). Also: chunk
lidský — human (of or belonging to the species Homo sapiens). Also: human, humane
mládí — youth (quality or state of being young).
nadále — still (at the time, no less than before). Also: henceforth
obchod — trade (commercial exchange of goods and services). Also: deal (instance of buying or selling), shop
poměr — ratio (number). Also: relationship, nexus, affair (adulterous relationship)
pravý — right (of direction). Also: right (of angle), true, genuine, right, proper
průvodce — guide (document, book). Also: wizard (program or script used to simplify complex operations)
rána — wound (injury). Also: bang (a sudden percussive noise)
rozhlas — radio. Also: echo, resonance, response
řád — order (opposite to chaos). Also: order, order (religious group)
studie — study (particular investigation such as a clinical trial).
studium — study (mental effort to acquire knowledge or learning).
stupeň — stair (a single step in a staircase). Also: degree, level, grade, stage, phase, degree, extent, degree
svaz — union (confederation; union of human organizations). Also: lattice
světlo — light (illumination produced by Sun or other sources).
Sýrie — Syria (a country in West Asia in the Middle East).
šaty — dress (item of clothing usually worn by a woman or young girl). Also: clothes, clothing
šťastný — happy (of a person). Also: lucky
titul — title (added to a person's name). Also: publication, book, title
trať — track (path or course laid out for a race, for exercise, etc.). Also: track (rails)
třída — class (group sharing attributes). Also: class (group of students taught together), classroom, class (category of seats in an airplane or train), class
tvar — shape (appearance or outline). Also: shape (figure)
úroveň — level (degree or amount). Also: level, level, stage
vedlejší — minor (of little significance or importance). Also: neighbouring
věž — tower. Also: rook, sail (of a submarine)
vstup — entry (the act of entering). Also: entry (a means of entering a building, an area or other facility), admission, input, accession, joining
zdejší — local (from or in a nearby location).
zelená — green. Also: peppermint spirit, vert
zem — earth. Also: country (nation state or a political entity)

aktivní — active (having the power or quality of acting; causing change; communicating action or motion; acting). Also: top (man penetrating or with a preference for penetrating during homosexual intercourse)
areál — campus (grounds or property of an organization, often including buildings).
bezpečnost — safety. Also: security (staff), safeness (of a quality)
blíže — closer, comparative degree of blízko.
dodat — to add. Also: to supply, to provide, to furnish, to deliver
efekt — effect (result). Also: effect (illusion produced by technical means), effect (scientific phenomenon, usually named after its discoverer)
Evropa — Europe (a continent located west of Asia and north of Africa). Also: European Union
Francie — France (a country located primarily in Western Europe; official name: Francouzská republika).
hmotnost — weight. Also: mass (quantity of matter which a body contains)
hráč — player (of a game). Also: player (on a musical instrument)

jazyk — tongue (the fleshy muscular organ in the mouth of a mammal). Also: a thing resembling a tongue, language (a method of interhuman communication), language, tongue (the conventional system of communication used by a particular community), language (the parlance of a particular specialist field), language (a particular style or manner of expression; idiom)
jednat — to act. Also: to negotiate, to treat, to concern, to be about
jednoduchý — simple (not compound). Also: simple (easy to understand or do)
jednoduše — easily (without difficulty). Also: just, simply
jízdní — riding (having to do with a ride).

klient — client (customer a buyer or receiver of goods or services). Also: client (computer application or system that remotely takes advantage of a server application or a system), client (person who receives help or advice from a professional person)
koule — sphere (solid). Also: ball, shot (for shot put), (in plural)
kurz — course (period of learning). Also: course (direction of movement of a vessel), rate (in exchange rate)
listy — leaves, a suit in German playing cards.
Londýn — London (the capital city of the United Kingdom; the capital city of England).
matka — mother. Also: nut (of a bolt)
metoda — method (process by which a task is completed). Also: method
mistr — master (expert at something). Also: master (tradesman who is qualified to teach apprentices), champion (someone who has been winner in a contest)
Moskva — Moscow (a federal city, the capital of Russia).
návrat — return (act of a person returning).
nebezpečí — danger. Also: peril (something that causes danger)
německý — German (relating to the country of Germany or to the German language).
objem — volume (unit of three dimensional measure).
paráda — parade. Also: great, awesome, neat, cool
platit — to pay (to give money). Also: to hold (to be true)
Polsko — Poland (a country in Central Europe).
portál — portal (grandiose and often lavish entrance).
postoj — stance (the way of holding a body). Also: stance (point of view)
princ — prince (male descendant of a monarch).
předmět — object (thing). Also: object (noun phrase which is an internal complement of a verb phrase or a prepositional phrase), subject (area of study), subject (of a mail)
příjem — income. Also: intake, reception (of radio or similar signals), reception (of a serve)
přítel — friend. Also: supporter, relative, relation (someone in the same family), boyfriend, lover
ptát — to ask (to request an answer).
recenze — review (account intended as a critical evaluation of a text or a piece of work).
recept — recipe. Also: prescription (physician's written order)
roční období — season (quarter of the year).
Rusko — Russia (a transcontinental country in Eastern Europe and North Asia; official name: Ruská federace).
Řecko — Greece (a country in Southeast Europe; official name: Řecká republika).
řeč — speech (faculty). Also: execution or performance of (1), speech, oration, language
říkat — to say. Also: to call, to name
scénář — script (performing arts). Also: scenario, screenplay (recorded media)
sedět — to sit (to be in a sitting position). Also: to fit (of clothing)
sestra — sister (sibling). Also: nurse (caring for the sick), nun, sister
snažit — to try (to work on something). Also: to pursue (to aim at)

užít — (transitive) to use, to take of medicine [with accusative ‘something’; or with genitive ‘’] (higher register with genitive). Also: to enjoy [with accusative ‘something’; or with genitive ‘’] (higher register with genitive)
Vídeň — Vienna (the capital city and state of Austria).
volně — freely. Also: loosely, voluntarily, willingly, gladly
výpověď — notice (notification of ending a contract). Also: testimony
výška — height. Also: altitude (the distance measured perpendicularly from a figure's vertex to the opposite side of the vertex), pitch (how high or low a musical tone, voice etc is)
vzadu — behind (at the back part; in the rear).
záznam — record. Also: record, entry, recording
zdaleka — by far. Also: from afar
zelené — leaves, a suit in German playing cards.
zpěvák — singer (person who sings, is able to sing, or earns a living by singing).
akademie — academy (society of leaned men). Also: academy (school or place of training)
alkohol — alcohol (any of a class of organic compounds (such as ethanol) containing a hydroxyl functional group (-OH)). Also: alcohol (intoxicating beverage)
Anglie — England (a constituent country of the United Kingdom).

baterie — battery (storage unit for electricity). Also: battery (coordinated group of artillery), tap ((bathroom) tap - device for dispensing liquids), battery (set of small cages for keeping of poultry), an array of bottles
brát — to take. Also: to marry (to be joined together as spouses), to marry, to help yourself (to some food)

Británie — Britain (the United Kingdom, a kingdom and country in Northern Europe including the island of Great Britain as well as Northern Ireland on the northeastern portion of the island of Ireland). Also: Britain (great Britain, a large island (sometimes also including some of the surrounding smaller islands) off the north-west coast of Western Europe, made up of England, Scotland, and Wales; especially (but not exclusively) during antiquity)
cítit — to feel (to use the sense of touch). Also: to smell (to sense a smell or smells), to feel
Čechy — Bohemia (a cultural region in the west of the former Czechoslovakia and present-day Czech Republic). Also: Czech Republic (a country in Central Europe)
čekat — to wait. Also: to expect
člen — element (in a sequence). Also: article; element
čtvrt — quarter (one of four equal parts).
dát pusu — to kiss (to touch with the lips).
digitální — digital (representing discrete values). Also: digital (done using a finger), digital (of or belonging to the finger)
dívat — to look (to try to see). Also: to watch
doprava — traffic. Also: transport, shipping (the cost of sending goods)
druh — species. Also: a type of something
držet — to hold. Also: to hold on, to hold tight, to stay, to remain
generál — general (military). Also: general: a xiangqi piece that is moved one point orthogonally and confined within the palace.
historie — history (aggregate of past events). Also: history (branch of learning), history, story
chtít — to want. Also: to fancy, to feel like
jet — to ride. Also: to go (by vehicle)

Komory — Comoros (a country and group of islands in the Indian Ocean off the coast of East Africa). Also: Comoros (an archipelago consisting of the Union of the Comoros and the French overseas department of Mayotte)
koncert — concert (musical entertainment in which several voices or instruments take part).
manžel — husband (a man in a marriage).
měsíční — monthly. Also: lunar (of, or pertaining to, the moon)
milovat — to love. Also: to make love
míra — measure (quantity, size, weight, distance or capacity of a substance compared to a designated standard). Also: measure

Morava — Moravia (a historic region in the eastern Czech Republic). Also: Morava (a left tributary of the Danube in Central Europe, flowing through Moravia in the Czech Republic on the border with Slovakia and then along the border of Austria), Morava, Great Morava (a river in central Serbia; in full, Great Morava)

Německo — Germany (a country in Central Europe, formed in 1949 as West Germany, with its provisional capital Bonn until 1990, when it incorporated East Germany; official name: Spolková republika Německo).
obsah — content (of a physical container). Also: table of contents, contents, area
podívat — to look (to try to see). Also: to have a look
pojem — concept. Also: term (word or phrase)
produkt — product (commodity for sale). Also: product
profil — profile (side view of a head).
přečíst — to read. Also: to peruse (to read completely)

questionTemplates.questionFormat: Which word is closest in meaning to '{targetLanguageWord}'?
